name: Asbestos Workers Local 14
description_short: Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management's asbestos information and regulation program providing guidance on asbestos identification, abatement, and safe disposal for residential and commercial properties.
description_long: The Asbestos program is operated by the Rhode Island Department of Environmental Management (RIDEM) and Rhode Island Department of Health (RIDOH) as a public regulatory and educational resource. It provides information about asbestos as a heat-resistant fibrous silicate mineral historically used in insulation, roofing, and coating materials, along with its health effects and regulatory requirements. The program offers comprehensive guidance on asbestos abatement rules, worker safety certification, proper transportation and disposal procedures, and distinctions between residential and commercial property regulations. What distinguishes this resource is its role as a state regulatory authority that certifies and licenses asbestos training courses, analytical labs, abatement contractors, and consultants—making it an authoritative source rather than a commercial service provider. The program is particularly valuable for Rhode Island property owners, contractors, and facilities managers needing compliant disposal options, including free limited asbestos waste disposal through RIRRC's Eco Depot. An honest assessment recognizes this is a government informational resource, not a commercial financial services provider; it offers regulatory compliance information and limited disposal assistance rather than financial products or credit services, and its applicability is restricted to Rhode Island residents and commercial entities.
services: Asbestos identification and health effects information
Certification and licensing of asbestos training courses
Certification and licensing of analytical laboratories for asbestos testing
Certification and licensing of asbestos abatement contractors
Certification and licensing of asbestos consultants
Free limited asbestos waste disposal via RIRRC Eco Depot (scheduling appointments)
Regulatory guidance for commercial properties, apartment buildings, and schools
Asbestos control rules and worker safety regulations
USDOT hazardous materials transportation compliance information
Disposal facility authorization and out-of-state disposal guidance
Referrals to RIDOH Asbestos Control Program resources
Information on friable vs. non-friable asbestos regulations
pros: Provides FREE limited asbestos waste disposal appointments through RIRRC Eco Depot (up to four bags per appointment)
Certifies and licenses asbestos abatement contractors and consultants, ensuring quality standards for consumers
Offers clear regulatory distinctions between commercial and residential asbestos handling requirements
Provides transportation guidance compliant with USDOT hazardous materials regulations and PHMSA requirements
Directs homeowners to licensed professionals and training resources for safe abatement
Explains disposal facility requirements and out-of-state options when in-state disposal is unavailable
Offers free informational resources on asbestos health effects through USEPA and RIDOH links
cons: Applicable only to Rhode Island residents and properties—not available for other states
Does not directly provide asbestos abatement, testing, or contractor services—only regulatory oversight and referrals
Free disposal limited to private homeowners with maximum four bags; commercial entities must find licensed facilities
Rhode Island's only solid waste disposal facility (RIRRC) does not accept asbestos waste except through limited Eco Depot program
Asbestos disposal outside Rhode Island may require additional state-specific paperwork and adherence to varying facility requirements
best_for: Rhode Island homeowners with asbestos-containing materials seeking safe, compliant disposal options
Commercial property and apartment building owners needing certified contractors and regulatory compliance guidance
School facility managers and administrators managing asbestos abatement projects
Contractors and consultants requiring licensing, certification, and regulatory requirement information
diagnosis: This is a government regulatory information resource, not a consumer finance company. It is best for Rhode Island property owners and commercial entities needing asbestos compliance, testing, contractor referrals, and limited disposal assistance. The main caveat is that it provides regulatory and informational services only—not financial products, credit services, or commercial abatement work—and is restricted to Rhode Island jurisdiction.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
